entry.title: Can Joule work with Situation handling in s/4 Public?
entry.content:
Dear all,

We are currently exploring the possibility of using Situation Handling to monitor job status in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. However, based on discussions with SAP experts, we understand that this would require either an add-on (e.g., creating a custom CDS View) or integration with SAP Cloud ALM.

At SAP SAPPHIRE 2026, we were informed that Joule will provide real-time job monitoring capabilities and can potentially work in conjunction with Situation Handling to proactively notify end users.

In this context, we would like to confirm the following:

  • Can Joule be integrated with Situation Handling?
  • If such integration is available, would it eliminate the need for additional developments (such as custom CDS Views) for job monitoring within Situation Handling, given Joule’s real-time monitoring capabilities?

We would appreciate your clarification on this matter.

m if Joule can work with Situation Handling ?

If so, it is no need to add-on in situation handling cause Joule has the real-time jobs monitoring capability .

entry.title: SAP Business AI | What | How | Insights
entry.content:
SAP Business AI

SAP Business AI is a comprehensive suite of artificial intelligence technologies and capabilities embedded directly into SAP's enterprise applications and business processes. It represents SAP's strategic approach to delivering relevant, reliable, and responsible AI that transforms how businesses operate by providing intelligent automation, predictive insights, and enhanced decision-making capabilities across the entire enterprise.

Key Components

  • Joule: SAP's AI-powered copilot that provides a unified, conversational interface for interacting with SAP applications using natural language.
  • Embedded AI: AI capabilities natively integrated into core SAP solutions like S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Ariba, providing out-of-the-box functionality.
  • AI Foundation: Built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), providing the underlying infrastructure for AI development, deployment, and governance.
  • Joule Agents: Specialized AI agents that handle complex, cross-functional business tasks autonomously or collaboratively.

Primary Capabilities

  • Intelligent Automation: Automates repetitive tasks like invoice processing, document analysis, and workflow management using machine learning and robotic process automation.
  • Predictive Analytics: Leverages machine learning algorithms to forecast trends, predict demand, and enable proactive decision-making across business functions.
  • Natural Language Processing: Enables conversational interactions, document processing, and intelligent content generation through advanced NLP capabilities.
  • Generative AI: Creates new content, summarizes complex information, generates code, and provides creative solutions for business challenges.

Integration with SAP Ecosystem

  • Deep Application Integration: AI capabilities are embedded across SAP's entire product portfolio including S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba, and SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Unified Data Foundation: Built on SAP BTP with services like SAP Datasphere and Integration Suite to ensure high-quality data access for AI algorithms.
  • Cross-Application Workflows: Joule provides seamless navigation and task execution across multiple SAP applications through a single interface.
  • Strategic Partnerships: Collaborates with leading AI vendors like Microsoft, Google, AWS, and IBM to leverage cutting-edge technologies and large language models.

Use Cases

  • Finance: Automated invoice processing, cash forecasting, financial reconciliation, and predictive analytics for better financial planning .
  • Supply Chain: Demand forecasting, predictive material requirements planning, and intelligent supply chain optimization.
  • Human Resources: Resume screening, job description generation, personalized learning recommendations, and workforce planning.
  • Procurement: Intelligent spend analysis, contract intelligence, supplier risk detection, and automated sourcing processes .
  • Customer Experience: Personalized recommendations, intelligent customer service, and enhanced user experiences across touchpoints .

Some useful resources

discovering-sap-business-ai

Business AI & Joule

ai-catalog

entry.title: Using SAP Joule with A2A from a custom agent on SAP BTP CF – any guidance or examples?
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

I am currently investigating how to use SAP Joule together with a custom agent running on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry.

 

What I would like to achieve is the following:

 

- Build a custom agent on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry

- Use A2A communication between my custom agent and SAP Joule

- Let SAP Joule handle specific SAP S/4HANA business operations, such as document creation or other standard S/4HANA transactions

- Keep the custom agent responsible for orchestration, while using Joule only for selected S/4HANA-related actions

 

I have found information about scenarios where Joule can call external/custom agents via A2A, but I am not sure whether the opposite direction is currently supported, meaning:

 

Custom Agent on BTP CF → A2A → SAP Joule → SAP S/4HANA operation

 

My questions are:

 

  1. Is this architecture currently supported or generally available?
  2. If yes, are there any official documents, tutorials, sample implementations, or reference architectures for this use case?

 

If this direction is not currently supported, I would also appreciate any recommended alternative architecture, for example using Joule as the entry point and calling a custom BTP CF agent from Joule.

 

Any guidance, documentation links, or practical experiences would be very helpful.

entry.title: How to limit the users to use Joule Base
entry.content:
Dear SAP specialists,

One of my customers uses Joule Base in SAP Cloud ERP development environment for PoC and is now planning to enable it in production environment.

Before that, the leader of IT department has requested that Joule Base should be made available only to a limited group of users, at least during the initial production rollout.

Is it possible to restrict Joule Base usage to specific users only in SAP Cloud ERP?
As I understand, Joule Base (pop-up to input prompts) can be used by every user who can access the SAP Cloud ERP.
If it is possible, can you let me know the way?

Thanks and best regards,

entry.title: Can an SAP Joule Agent be invoked programmatically?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are wondering whether it is possible to invoke an SAP Joule Agent programmatically.

Our use case is fairly straightforward: we have a CAP application with a UI where users can provide input. We would like to pass that input to a Joule Agent and have the agent perform actions in other systems or processes. To achieve this, we would need a way to access or invoke the agent programmatically from Node.js, Python etc.

Is there currently a way to invoke Joule Agents programmatically? If not, are there any plans on SAP's roadmap to provide APIs or other integration mechanisms for communicating with Joule Agents?

Best,

entry.title: Joule with Document Grounding connecting to Sharepoint Site
entry.content: We are reviewing the Document Grounding with Joule processes trying to connect to a Sharepoint site and we were curious to get some insights on other customers who have set this up and if they were able to allow Joule to pull data from Sharepoint pages since we do not documents loaded on our Sharepoint site as all the content are loaded on the pages.

entry.title: Joule Booster – "Setting Up Subaccount(s)" Step FAILED | SAP BTP - public cloud
entry.content:
Hello ,

I am trying to set up Joule using the SAP BTP Unified Booster for Joule provisioning, but the booster is failing during execution.

---

ISSUE SUMMARY

While running the Joule Booster in SAP BTP, the progress dialog shows:
• Setting up subaccount(s) → FAILED
• Setting up integrations → SKIPPED

Error message: "Setup encountered issues and could not be completed."

 

It created joule subscription but never created any formations/destinations. 

I'm trying to setup with Build work zone standard edition foundation plan.

@George_Yu1 @LilingJia @nageshcaparthy

Thanks

entry.title: Generating Technical Specification Document using SAP Joule
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to generate a Technical Specification Word Document using SAP BTP Joule Skill, and Joule Agent but we are not getting desired output. Is anyone tried generating the tech spec using SAP Joule? if yes, can anyone please help us what's best approach to be used to achieve this? and also, we are facing below error ::

Error: Due to system limitations not able to generate.

Regards,

Kalpa B

entry.title: AI Agent user Licensing
entry.content:
In most enterprises, only a small subset of employees have SAP user licenses and direct access to SAP systems. However, AI Agents are expected to be democratized and accessible to a much broader employee base across the organization, with use cases varying according to each employee's role and responsibilities.

In such a scenario, how does SAP licensing work for enterprise-wide AI Agent usage? Specifically, if employees who do not possess individual SAP user licenses interact with SAP data or processes through AI Agents, what are the SAP user licensing implications and compliance considerations?

ex. if a customer has Workday (all employees will have access to it as its HR) .. if I want to implement a AI agent use-case for Workday using SAP AI core.. what would be the license impact ?

entry.title: How AI is affecting SAP world in day-to-day work
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I'm trying to understand how AI is changing the work of SAP consultants and developers in real world projects.

I'd appreciate hearing about your experience with AI tools in your respective domains.

  • Which AI tools do you use regularly? (Joule, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, Claude, etc)
  • What kind of tasks became easier for you because of these tools?
  • Which technologies are your clients or companies adopting?
  • Which AI skills do you think SAP professionals should learn over the next few years?

    I'm interested in practical experiences rather than marketing information.

entry.title: Is it possible to query Joule via an API?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I'm exploring the possibility of integrating SAP Joule with an external system.
My question is: does SAP currently expose an API to send queries/prompts to Joule programmatically and receive its responses, similar to how one would call a generative AI endpoint?
Specifically, I'd like to understand:

Is there an official API (REST or otherwise) to interact with Joule outside of its native UI?
If so, what licensing or SAP BTP setup is required to access it?
Are there any current limitations on which Joule capabilities (e.g., Joule for SAP S/4HANA vs. Joule Studio/custom skills) can be accessed this way?
Is this documented anywhere, or is it still in early access/roadmap stage?

Any guidance, documentation links, or experiences from those who've tried this would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Joule Artificial Intelligence

entry.title: Joule skill- Error EL1008E: Property or field 'result' cannot be found on object of type 'Map'
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am facing this issue where I am getting the error while testing the Joule skill-
EL1008E: Property or field 'result' cannot be found on object of type 'Map' - maybe not public or not valid?

The skill tester is returning null values but the same when tested from postman works fine. Not sure what is the issue here. Could you please point me to the right direction. 


1.  Test Results

a.

b. API call status

c. Output fields are null

3. Joule skill

Input parameters

Output  parameters

4. The same API is working fine when tested from postman.

Thanks and Regards,
Rudra
Joule Studio, classic edition Joule SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: SAP's agentic moment is here but a few practical questions I keep hearing
entry.content:
Watching the Autonomous Enterprise vision come together since Sapphire has been genuinely exciting. Joule Studio hitting GA, the A2A protocol opening up, Claude in the reasoning layer, SAP has done something important: it's made "agents that execute, not just assist" the default expectation for ERP. That's a rising tide for everyone working in this space.

(Quick disclosure: I'm the founder of agentnomics.ai, where we build a vendor-neutral agent layer for SAP, so I'm in this every day.)

What I find interesting is the set of practical questions this surge is surfacing for smaller and mid-market SAP shops, the teams I spend most of my time with:

  • Cost predictability. Consumption-based AI Units are flexible, but a lot of finance leaders are finding the annual number hard to forecast, especially with overage rates and expiring credits. Budgeting for agents shouldn't require an ITAM owner watching SAP for me every month.
  • Estates that aren't clean cloud S/4HANA yet. Joule is strongest on the newest landscapes. Plenty of healthy businesses are still on ECC or heavily customized hybrid setups and want agentic value now, via OData/RFC, without a full migration first.
  • Life beyond SAP. Most teams don't live only in SAP they're also in Salesforce, ServiceNow, and commerce tools like Shopify and Square. The dream is one agent across all of it.

The approach we've taken is to meet teams where they are: flat-rate so the cost is the cost, model-agnostic so you're not married to one LLM's pricing, secure governed write-back across SAP and non-SAP systems, and live in weeks rather than quarters. It's complementary to where SAP is heading, just aimed squarely at the cost-conscious mid-market.

Genuinely curious how others here are thinking about it: if you're rolling out agents on ECC or hybrid, how are you handling cost forecasting and cross-system reach?

entry.title: Joule Work on RISE — do SAP GUI users need to move to Fiori, or only for the embedded Joule Assistan
entry.content:
We're a RISE with SAP (S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition) customer, and most users still work in classic SAP GUI, not Fiori.

My understanding is that the embedded Joule Assistant is surfaced inside the UI, so that would require Fiori — but Joule Work is a separate, standalone agentic workspace that executes against the backend via
APIs, independent of the user's transactional screen.

So my question is: for Joule Work specifically, can our SAP GUI users keep using GUI as-is, or do they need to migrate to Fiori?

Thanks!

entry.title: Intent Based Navigation with appSpecificRoute
entry.content:
Hi Colleagues,

We are developing navigation out of a Joule Capability, using intent based navigation. Our goal is to target an application with a specified object open already, ie, internal app navigation.

The target URL would be such as:
#IBPApplicationLog-display&/LogDetails/9FB4E95040ED6EF01900EADA8D2895A4

However, the documentation of Card Navigation Actions or navigation action handling do not mention anything about the app specific route parameter handling.

If i check the Navigation services API, i can easily achieve the goal by doing the following in the console:
oNavService = sap.ushell.Container.getService("Navigation"); 
...

oNavService .navigate({
target: {
semanticObject: "IBPApplicationLog",
action: "display",
},
appSpecificRoute: "LogDetails/9FB4E95040ED6EF01900EADA8D2895A4"
})
 
But we aren't sure how to pass such parameters, since the documentation only describes ibnTarget and ibnParams. Is there any description that tells us the syntax necessary to achieve our target url navigation?
 
Thank You and Best Regards,
Mihaly

entry.title: Can SAP Joule Skills Be Triggered by External Events (e.g., Incoming Email)?
entry.content:
I would like to understand whether SAP Joule Skills can be triggered automatically based on external events, rather than being invoked manually by a user.

For example, consider the following scenario:

  • An email arrives in a shared mailbox (e.g., Outlook or Microsoft 365).
  • This email should automatically trigger a Joule Skill.
  • The skill processes the email content, performs the required business logic, and optionally creates or updates business objects in an SAP system.

Is this type of event-driven invocation currently supported for Joule Skills?

entry.title: No authorization to access service group API_PROJECT_V3
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are currently trying to configure Joule for S/4 Hana 2023 PCE system. While testing transactional capability - Open Project, WBS Element, and Networks in Object Pages or the Project Builder Application via Joule, we encountered an issue and SAP suggested to activate API API_PROJECT_V3. After activation, we are getting additional error: No authorization to access service group API_PROJECT_V3 in the /IWFND/ERROR_LOG.

SU53 shows S_START missing authorization for R3TR G4BA API_PROJECT_V3.

Currently this access is not present in any of our custom roles. Do we need to add access to this API in our current set of roles ?

Please advise.

Regards,

Geetika Sood

entry.title: Joule Custom MDF Redirection Request
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have activated Joule integrated with SuccessFactors.
We would like Joule to be able to redirect users' profile-related requests to custom MDF objects available within the employee profile.

Could you please advise which integration, configuration, or additional component needs to be implemented to enable this type of functionality with Joule or can explain us step about this?

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Cannot Find my deployed newly created custom joule agent in SAP ALM or SAP LEANIX
entry.content:
Hello,

I have developed and deployed (to a shared environment) a new Joule agent and skill. But i am trying to manage this agent via AI agent hub in SAP LeanIX. So i followed the help docs to connect my SAP ALM instance to my btp instance, then my LeanIX to my SAP ALM instance. But so far i can see everything in ALM except my joule agent that i have deployed.

 

I am just trying to evaluate AI agent hub in leanIX in managing custom developed joule agents.

entry.title: Building a custom MCP server on SAP BTP Trial account for SAP Document AI
entry.content:
BTP Trail: Building a custom MCP server on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry

Deploying a real MCP server — with SAP Document AI tools behind it — on a BTP trial account.

MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets an LLM client call your backend as tools instead of you gluing together prompts and API calls by hand. This post deploys one on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, secures it with XSUAA, and exposes SAP Document AI (schemas, extraction, enrichment) as scoped tools any MCP client — Claude, ChatGPT, MCP Inspector, and eventually SAP Joule — can call. 

In this post:

  1. Architecture
  2. Tools, languages, and BTP services (with download links)
  3. Deploying it step by step (including getting the code and making it yours)
  4. What you can do once it's running

1. Architecture

Four layers, top to bottom:

Layer What lives there

MCP clientsClaude, ChatGPT connectors, MCP Inspector, a custom CLI, and — architecturally ready for — SAP Joule. All speak the same Streamable HTTP transport against one /mcp route.
OAuth 2.1 entry pointEvery call except a small set of discovery routes requires a bearer token. This layer is where nearly all the non-obvious engineering effort went.
Cloud Foundry app (learning-mcp-server)A FastMCP application: one tool per SAP action, a generic auth middleware in front of all of them, and a lightweight in-memory store tracking Document AI submissions.
BTP backend servicesXSUAA validates identity and scopes; SAP Document AI is called downstream with its own independent client-credentials token.

 

2. Tools, languages, and BTP services

Languages / runtime

  • Python 3.12
  • Node.js + npm — only needed for npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector and the mcp-remote bridge Claude Desktop uses to reach a remote server

Python packages

Package Role

mcp[cli]FastMCP server framework and MCP protocol types
httpxHTTP client for all outbound BTP API calls
sap-xssecValidates XSUAA-issued JWTs, exposes scope checks
cfenvReads bound service credentials (VCAP_SERVICES) at runtime

CLI tools

  • Cloud Foundry CLI (cf) — login, push, restage, service management
  • MCP Inspector — the standard way to exercise a server's tools before pointing a real LLM client at it

BTP services

Service Purpose

Cloud Foundry Environment (trial plan)Hosts the app
XSUAA (application plan)Issues/validates OAuth tokens; carries the scopes and role collections from xs-security.json
SAP Document AI (Document Information Extraction)The business capability exposed as tools: schemas, submission, extraction, enrichment

Document AI credentials are deliberately not part of the XSUAA binding — they're a separate service key, injected via cf set-env and kept out of manifest.yml so a routine cf push never overwrites them with blanks.

2.1 Download links

Tool Link

SAP BTP trial signupsap.com/products/technology-platform/trial.html
Cloud Foundry CLIgithub.com/cloudfoundry/cli/releases
Node.js + npmnodejs.org
Python 3.12python.org/downloads
MCP Inspector (via npx, no separate install)npmjs.com/package/@modelcontextprotocol/inspector
mcp[cli] (Python)pypi.org/project/mcp
sap-xssecpypi.org/project/sap-xssec
cfenvpypi.org/project/cfenv
httpxpypi.org/project/httpx
SAP Document AI (Document Information Extraction) API docshelp.sap.com/docs/document-ai
MCP specificationmodelcontextprotocol.io
Claude Desktopclaude.ai/download

3. Deploying it step by step

3.1 Prerequisites

  1. Sign up for a BTP trial account (link above) — you'll need an SAP account; the trial itself is free with no time-limited data beyond the 90-day window.
  2. In the cockpit, open your subaccount → Cloud Foundry Environment. If it's not already enabled, click Enable Cloud Foundry, choose the free/trial plan, keep the defaults, and create it. A default space (usually dev) is created automatically — you can add more spaces later if you want to separate environments.
  3. Note the API Endpoint shown in the cockpit (something like https://api.cf.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com) — you'll need it to log in.
  4. Install the Cloud Foundry CLI (cf) from the link above, or via a package manager (e.g. brew install cloudfoundry/tap/cf-cli on macOS). Verify with cf --version.
  5. Install Node.js + npm (needed only for npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector and the mcp-remote bridge Claude Desktop uses).
  6. Install Python 3.12 locally if you want to run the CLI test client or iterate on tool logic outside Cloud Foundry.

3.2 Get the code and make it your own

Full source: github.com/Sharan-cypto/SAP-Document-AI

Clone or download the source:

git clone https://github.com/Sharan-cypto/SAP-Document-AI.git  #Extract the file
cd SAP-Document-AI

Before your first cf push, make these changes :

  1. manifest.yml — rename the app (name:) and the service instance under services: to whatever you want to call your own deployment.
  2. xs-security.json — set xsappname to match the app name you just chose. The redirect-uris can stay as-is (they're generic MCP client callbacks, not tied to any account) unless you're adding a different client.
  3. Your own Document AI service key — you'll need this in step 3.4, so it's worth having it open now: in the BTP cockpit, open the Document AI service instance → Service Keys → create or view one. It's a JSON blob containing uaa.url, uaa.clientid, uaa.clientsecret, and the API base url — never type these directly into any file, they're passed in as environment variables in step 3.4.

3.3 Log in and do a first, unauthenticated push

Trial accounts use SSO passcode login rather than a password:

cf login -a <API-ENDPOINT> --sso

This opens a URL (or prints one to paste into a browser) where you generate a one-time passcode, then paste it back at the Temporary Authentication Code prompt. Once authenticated, you'll be asked to pick an org (your trial org, usually named after your subaccount) and then a space — pick dev unless you've created a separate one, or press enter to skip and target it later with cf target -o <org> -s <space>.

On CMD, From the project folder path:

cf push

This reads manifest.yml, builds with the Python buildpack, and starts the app, printing a route like learning-mcp-server-<random>.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com — your MCP endpoint is <that route>/mcp.

At this point the server has no authentication. That's fine for a first smoke test with MCP Inspector; it's never acceptable once real SAP data sits behind it.

3.4 Wire up SAP Document AI

Export the Document AI service key's credentials — gathered in step 3.2 — as environment variables, never plaintext in manifest.yml:

cf set-env learning-mcp-server DOCAI_UAA_URL '<uaa url from service key>'
cf set-env learning-mcp-server DOCAI_CLIENT_ID '<clientid from service key>'
cf set-env learning-mcp-server DOCAI_CLIENT_SECRET '<clientsecret from service key>'
cf set-env learning-mcp-server DOCAI_API_URL '<Document AI API base url>'
cf restart learning-mcp-server

Verify they landed correctly with cf env learning-mcp-server.

3.5 Add XSUAA-backed OAuth (the part that takes iteration)

  1. Write xs-security.json — one scope per permission level (e.g. docai.read, docai.submit, docai.schema.write, docai.document.delete, docai.enrichment.write, btp.admin), grouped into role templates and then role collections. Also declare the redirect URIs your MCP clients will use (Claude, ChatGPT connectors, MCP Inspector's local callback, etc.).
  2. Create the XSUAA instance from that file:
    cf create-service xsuaa application learning-mcp-xsuaa -c xs-security.json
  3. Bind it and restage:
    cf bind-service learning-mcp-server learning-mcp-xsuaacf restage learning-mcp-server
  4. Whenever xs-security.json changes:
    cf update-service learning-mcp-xsuaa -c xs-security.jsoncf restage learning-mcp-server
  5. Assign a role collection (e.g. "DocAI Operator") to your BTP user in the cockpit. Skip this and an otherwise-valid token still gets 403s on every scoped tool.

The problem this solves: MCP clients expect an authorization server to support Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591) and fail immediately with oauth_error=registration_endpoint_missing if it doesn't — XSUAA has no such endpoint. Here's the handshake that fixes it without touching XSUAA itself:

 

3.6 Verify, then connect a real client

npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector

Choose Streamable HTTP, paste https://<your-route>/mcp, connect — this now exercises the full OAuth + DCR flow, not an open endpoint.

Once that works, connect a real client — see section 4 for a worked example connecting Claude Desktop.

3.7 Operating commands

cf logs learning-mcp-server --recent   # crash cause / last errors
cf logs learning-mcp-server            # tail live logs
cf apps                                # deployed apps, routes, state
cf restart learning-mcp-server         # after env var changes
cf restage learning-mcp-server         # after service binding changes

4. What you can do once it's running

Example: connecting Claude Desktop Claude Desktop expects a local command rather than a remote URL, so bridge in the deployed server via mcp-remote. Add this to Claude Desktop's config file:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "learning-server": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "mcp-remote", "https://<your-route>/mcp"]
    }
  }
}

Restart Claude Desktop — it will walk you through the OAuth login against XSUAA on first use, then the server's tools show up like any other MCP connector. Other clients (ChatGPT connectors, MCP Inspector) connect directly to https://<your-route>/mcp without needing this bridge, since they speak Streamable HTTP natively.

OR

Directly connect MCP server to Claude Desktop:

   1. Once Login to CF, Execute : cf env learning-mcp-server  -- Note down clientid & clientsecret

   2. On Claude Desktop, File-->Settings--> Connectors--> Add Custom Connectors & Provide the Details and             Connect.

Once connected, you just talk to your MCP client (Claude, ChatGPT, MCP Inspector, or eventually Joule) in plain language — it picks the right tool behind the scenes. That covers the full range of SAP Document AI capabilities:

  • Discover — check what document types, fields, and schemas are available
  • Design schemas — create, edit, activate/deactivate, export, or import a custom schema
  • Submit — extract a single document or a batch; if more than one schema fits, the client asks which one to use (MCP elicitation — the conversational equivalent of the "Schema" dropdown in the Document AI web UI)
  • Track results — check job status, poll multiple jobs at once, or list everything submitted so far
  • Confirm or delete — lock in a reviewed extraction, or remove documents permanently
  • Manage master data — upload, view, or remove enrichment records (e.g. a vendor list) used to auto-match extracted fields

Every action maps to a scoped MCP tool — the caller's token must carry the matching scope, and job history is automatically filtered to the caller's own user_id unless they hold btp.admin.

Scope Role collection Covers

docai.readDocAI ViewerDiscovery, schema inspection, job history, polling results, exporting schemas
docai.submitDocAI OperatorSubmitting single or batch documents for extraction
docai.schema.writeDocAI Schema AdminCreating, editing, activating, and importing schemas
docai.document.deleteDocAI Operator / Schema AdminConfirming results, permanently deleting documents
docai.enrichment.writeDocAI Schema AdminUploading, listing, deleting master data

The btp.admin scope also has a separate effect: a caller who holds it sees every user's job history, not just their own — the one place in this build where a scope changes result visibility rather than just gating a tool call.

If integrated with SAP Joule

Joule would consume this server exactly like Claude or ChatGPT do today — same /mcp endpoint, same OAuth 2.1 + DCR flow, same scoped tool list. A business user could ask Joule to submit a document or check on pending invoices, and Joule would call the same tools shown here, with the same per-user scoping and audit trail, since the identity in the token doesn't change based on which client initiated the call.

 

Resources:

Document AI:

https://help.sap.com/docs/document-ai/sap-document-ai/what-is-sap-document-ai

Third-Party MCP:

https://architecture.learning.sap.com/docs/ref-arch/137800
